description.p: Per-Device EQ applies a parametric equaliser directly inside the real PipeWire sink, so every output device — speakers, a Bluetooth headset, a USB DAC — keeps its own correction. The EQ is written as an in-node filter graph, with no extra virtual sink in the audio path, and is restored automatically by a WirePlumber hook: it survives reboot and device reconnect with no background process of your own running.

ul.li: One saved correction per output device, applied and restored automatically
Interactive editor with a frequency-response graph, per-channel bands and a preamp
Import REW and AutoEQ filters; export and reuse profiles
Applies to every application, including pro-audio tools that open the device directly

screenshot.caption: Editing the equaliser for a Bluetooth headset

description.p: Packaging release: AppImage for non-Fedora distros joins every release
Measurement schema v4: per-take calibration passports, and the app arrives as a Flatpak

Every take now carries its own calibration -- rig stamps live on sessions, calibration curves in an append-only library -- so the statistics judge calibrated curves and recorded calibrations can be reassigned in bulk. Existing profiles convert once with the bundled migration tool.
The Flatpak ships scoped permissions, full PipeWire control from inside the sandbox, and a system-integration switch in the main menu: one-click install and removal, with a copyable restart command.

The app is the instrument now: built-in sweep measurement with incremental takes and automatic fitting, a taste layer composed over any profile on any device, honest headroom, and an adaptive measurement window.
